questionText: A curve is defined by $x = t + 1$ and $y = 2t$ for $0 \le t \le 4$. State the domain (the values of $x$) and the range (the values of $y$) of the curve.

steps.explanation: The parameter only runs from $0$ to $4$, so just part of the line is drawn. The extreme values of $x$ and $y$ come from the two ends of this interval.
Substituting the two end values of $t$ into $x = t + 1$ gives the smallest and largest $x$-values.
Because $x = t + 1$ increases steadily as $t$ increases, $x$ passes through every value between $1$ and $5$, giving the domain.
Substituting the same end values of $t$ into $y = 2t$ gives the smallest and largest $y$-values.
Since $y = 2t$ also increases with $t$, $y$ runs from $0$ up to $8$, giving the range.
Together these describe exactly the part of the line traced out as $t$ moves from $0$ to $4$.
